binaries.title: ntfs.sys - TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange RTL_BITMAP pool allocation rounded up to ULONG boundary (feature-staged; latent ~2-byte OOB bitmap access, CWE-131)

changed_functions.name: TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange
key_changes: Pool allocation size for the TOPS RTL_BITMAP structure changed from `(r14_3>>3)+0x58` (unpatched: `mov eax,r14d; shr eax,0x3; add ax,0x58; movzx eax,ax`) to a ULONG-rounded value `((r14_3>>3)+0x5b) & 0xfffffffc` in the patched binary.
New feature gate `Feature_NTFS_BR_TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRangeBitmapSizeAlignFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline()` selects between old (buggy) `+0x58` size and new aligned `+0x5b & ~3` size.

SizeOfBitMap for the RTL_BITMAP is stored at P[0x2a] (`mov word [r15+0x54], r14w`) with the bitmap buffer at P+0x58 (`lea rax,[r15+0x58]`); this bit count is only 16-bit aligned while Rtl bitmap APIs (RtlSetBits/RtlClearBit/RtlFindSetBits) operate on full 32-bit ULONG words.
Rest of diff (register renames r13->r12/r14->r15, ETW helper address changes) is recompilation noise.
changed_functions.security_notes: The function allocates a pool buffer embedding an RTL_BITMAP whose SizeOfBitMap (v14, bits) is always a multiple of 16 (both operands masked with 0xFFFFFFF0). The allocation size (v14>>3)+0x58 gives exactly v14/8 bytes of bitmap storage at offset 0x58 and is not rounded to a ULONG boundary, so when v14 % 32 == 16 the trailing bitmap ULONG extends ~2 bytes past the logical allocation. CORRECTION: only RtlSetBits (single-bit sets, in-bounds index) touches this buffer; RtlFindSetBits/RtlClearBit/RtlAreBitsClear operate on the separate Scb bitmap at a2+0x140. The trailing-word RMW writes the OOB bytes back unchanged (no attacker data written OOB), and 16-byte pool rounding absorbs the ~2 bytes on default systems (no adjacent-object corruption, no info leak). The access is observable only as a fault under Special Pool. The patch rounds the total allocation up to a 4-byte multiple so the trailing ULONG is fully backed; it is gated behind a WIL feature flag with the old path retained. Severity: Low (correctness/hardening), not the High exploitable pool overflow originally claimed.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- ntfs_unpatched.sys/TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange.assembly
+++ ntfs_patched.sys/TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange.assembly
@@ vulnerable allocation block (unpatched, '-' lines) @@
 lea     r14d, [rax+0x10]
 mov     dword [rsp+0x94], r14d
 sub     r14d, r12d
 mov     dword [rsp+0x94], r14d
 mov     eax, r14d
 shr     eax, 0x3
 add     ax, 0x58              ; <-- size = (bits/8)+0x58, NO ULONG rounding (16-bit add)
 movzx   eax, ax
 mov     qword [rsp+0xa8], rax
 mov     ecx, dword [rel 0x140097b20]
 or      ecx, 0x10
 mov     r8d, 0x3066744e       ; tag 'Ntf0'
 mov     edx, eax              ; allocation size
 call    qword [rel 0x1400ad3f0] ; ExAllocatePoolWithTag
 nop     dword [rax+rax]
 mov     r15, rax
 mov     qword [rsp+0xb0], rax
 mov     r8, qword [rsp+0xa8]
 xor     edx, edx
 mov     rcx, rax
 call    0x140058180           ; memset(P, 0, size)
 mov     word [r15], si
 mov     dword [r15+0x4], 0x15
 mov     eax, 0x58
 mov     word [r15+0x56], ax
 mov     word [r15+0x54], r14w  ; RTL_BITMAP.SizeOfBitMap = r14_3 (bits, only 16-aligned)
 mov     dword [r15+0x50], r12d
 lea     rax, [r15+0x58]       ; RTL_BITMAP.Buffer = P + 0x58
 mov     qword [rsp+0xc0], rax
 movzx   eax, word [r15+0x54]
 mov     dword [rsp+0xb8], eax
 mov     r14d, r12d
 mov     dword [rsp+0x98], r12d
@@ patched replacement ('+' lines) @@
+movzx   r14d, ax
+call    0x140051978           ; Feature_NTFS_BR_...BitmapSizeAlignFix IsEnabled
+test    eax, eax
+je      0x140103947           ; feature OFF -> old lea [r14+0x58]
+lea     rax, [r14+0x58]       ; OLD size (bug retained when flag off)
+lea     rax, [r14+0x5b]       ; NEW: +0x5b
+and     rax, 0xfffffffffffffffc ; & ~3  -> round allocation up to ULONG boundary

changed_functions.name: NtfsCommitCurrentTransaction
key_changes: ETW provider GUID symbol rename Microsoft_Windows_NtfsLog_1c495e8d...->98a93039... (full-binary recompile / version drift).
Branch-condition inversion with identical semantics (`if(rdx[3]&1) A else B` -> `if(!(rdx[3]&1)) B else A`).
Inline iso-snapshot cleanup loop over *(ctx+0x1a8) refactored into a call to TxfCleanupIsoSnapshots(); return value of NtfsProcessUsnRecordsForCommit/NtfsProcessNewLengthQueue no longer stored.

changed_functions.name: NtfsCheckpointForVolumeSnapshot
key_changes: The `if(!NtfsIsVcbAvailableForThisRequest)` error path and the `else` main-work path were swapped to `if(rax) main else error` — pure control-flow inversion, identical behavior.
ETW provider GUID rename and reordered NTSTATUS comparison chain (STATUS_CANT_WAIT/END_OF_FILE/PENDING ordering) — codegen noise.

changed_functions.name: NtfsCheckpointForVolumeSnapshot$fin$0
key_changes: Reordered NTSTATUS equality comparisons and moved the `rbx` non-zero check earlier in the debug-trace condition.
ETW logsup symbol offset renames — recompilation artifacts.

changed_functions.name: NtfsCheckpointVolume
key_changes: Large function; changes limited to register allocation and ETW GUID/logsup symbol renames consistent with full-binary recompile. No added bounds/validation checks observed relative to callers of the patched TxF path.

changed_functions.name: NtfsFlushLsnStreamsOnLogTooFull
key_changes: Only ETW logsup symbol offset renames (logsup_c1532->c1526, c1588->c1582) and basic-block address shifts. No behavioral change.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState
key_changes: WIL feature-state cache logic changed to accommodate the newly added Feature_NTFS_BR_TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRangeBitmapSizeAlignFix flag. Telemetry/feature-gating infrastructure, not a vulnerability itself.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_RegisterFeatureUsageProvider
key_changes: WIL feature-usage provider registration change tied to the new feature flag; infrastructure only.

security_findings.delivery: staged (WIL feature flag Feature_NTFS_BR_TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRangeBitmapSizeAlignFix; old unrounded path retained when the flag is off; runtime default not statically determinable)
security_findings.entry_point: IRP_MJ_WRITE (NtfsCommonWrite) — WriteFile against a TxF-transacted file/stream (file opened inside a KTM transaction via CreateFileTransacted); also reachable via IRP_MJ_SET_INFORMATION/EOF path (TxfSetAttrSize) and NtfsZeroRangeInStream.
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Incorrect buffer-size calculation (CWE-131): an embedded RTL_BITMAP's pool allocation is not rounded up to a 32-bit ULONG boundary, so the trailing bitmap word can extend ~2 bytes past the logical allocation. Latent out-of-bounds access (CWE-125/CWE-787 at the ULONG-granularity level). Correctness/hardening fix, not a demonstrable exploitable overflow.
security_findings.description: TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange builds a TOPS (TxF Old Page Stream) log-array record that embeds an RTL_BITMAP. It computes v13 = v5 & 0xFFFFFFF0, and a bit count v14 = ((v5+v4-1)&0xFFFFFFF0) + 16 - (v5&0xFFFFFFF0), which is always a multiple of 16. It allocates Size = (v14>>3)+0x58 bytes with ExAllocatePoolWithTag(tag 0x3066744E 'Ntf0'), stores SizeOfBitMap = v14 (bits) at P+0x54 and Buffer = P+0x58, and sets bits with RtlSetBits(&BitMapHeader, i-v13, 1) inside a loop. The allocation provides exactly v14/8 bytes of bitmap storage; because the Rtl bitmap helpers touch whole 32-bit ULONGs, when v14 % 32 == 16 the trailing ULONG of the bitmap extends ~2 bytes past the logical allocation end. IMPORTANT CORRECTIONS to the original engine report: (1) the ONLY Rtl operation on the undersized buffer is RtlSetBits (single-bit sets, index always in-bounds); RtlFindSetBits, RtlClearBit, and RtlAreBitsClear in this function operate on a DIFFERENT persistent Scb bitmap at a2+0x140 (asm: lea rcx,[rdi+0x28], rdi=a2+0x118), not on the allocation. (2) The trailing-word access is a read plus a value-preserving read-modify-write; the modified bit is always in-bounds, so no attacker-controlled data is written out of bounds. (3) ExAllocatePoolWithTag rounds allocations up to 16-byte granularity, so on production pool the ~2 trailing bytes fall inside the block's own rounding slack (minimum slack is exactly 2 bytes in the tightest case) and never reach an adjacent allocation. (4) The over-read feeds only internal bitmap logic and is not returned to user mode. Net: a latent out-of-bounds access observable only as a fault under Special Pool / Driver Verifier. The patch, gated by Feature_NTFS_BR_TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRangeBitmapSizeAlignFix, changes the size from ((v14>>3)+0x58) to (((v14>>3)+0x58+3) & ~3) so the final bitmap ULONG is fully backed; the old path is retained when the flag is off.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: A local user opens a file inside a KTM transaction (CreateTransaction + CreateFileTransacted) so writes route through the TxF old-page-stream flush path and reach TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange with a 16-aligned span v14 satisfying v14 & 0x1F == 0x10 (16, 48, 80, ...), with the entry RtlAreBitsClear(a2+0x140, a3, a4) returning FALSE so the allocation branch is taken. When that holds, RtlSetBits and a _bittest64 read touch the trailing ULONG at r15+0x58+((v14/8)&~3). Under Special Pool / Driver Verifier on tag 'Ntf0' this can fault against the guard page; without Special Pool the access lands in the allocation's 16-byte rounding slack with no observable effect. The exact WriteFile offset/length -> v14 mapping and reachability of the allocation branch were NOT proven end-to-end; they are inferred from the function arithmetic.
security_findings.primitive: Latent kernel non-paged-pool out-of-bounds access of up to ~2 bytes past a 'Ntf0'-tagged allocation: an over-read plus a value-preserving read-modify-write via RtlSetBits. NOT a controllable OOB write of attacker data. Contained within 16-byte pool rounding on default systems (no adjacent-object corruption, no info leak to user mode). Realistic worst case is a local fault/DoS only when Special Pool / Driver Verifier is enabled on tag 'Ntf0' (non-default). No pool grooming, adjacent-object corruption, arbitrary write, or privilege escalation is supported by the diff.
security_findings.assembly:
unpatched TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange @ 0x1401037f8 (vulnerable allocation block, verbatim):
lea     r14d, [rax+0x10]
mov     dword [rsp+0x94], r14d
sub     r14d, r12d                 ; r14_3 = ((r15-1+r14)&~0xf)+0x10 - (r14&~0xf)
mov     dword [rsp+0x94], r14d
mov     eax, r14d
shr     eax, 0x3                   ; eax = r14_3 / 8 (bitmap bytes)
add     ax, 0x58                   ; +0x58 header; 16-bit add, NO ULONG rounding  <-- BUG
movzx   eax, ax
mov     qword [rsp+0xa8], rax       ; allocation size
mov     ecx, dword [rel 0x140097b20]
or      ecx, 0x10
mov     r8d, 0x3066744e            ; pool tag 'Ntf0'
mov     edx, eax
call    qword [rel 0x1400ad3f0]     ; ExAllocatePoolWithTag
nop     dword [rax+rax]
mov     r15, rax
mov     qword [rsp+0xb0], rax
mov     r8, qword [rsp+0xa8]
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, rax
call    0x140058180                ; memset(P,0,size)
mov     word [r15], si
mov     dword [r15+0x4], 0x15
mov     eax, 0x58
mov     word [r15+0x56], ax
mov     word [r15+0x54], r14w       ; RTL_BITMAP.SizeOfBitMap = r14_3 (bits)
mov     dword [r15+0x50], r12d
lea     rax, [r15+0x58]            ; RTL_BITMAP.Buffer = P + 0x58
mov     qword [rsp+0xc0], rax
movzx   eax, word [r15+0x54]
mov     dword [rsp+0xb8], eax
mov     r14d, r12d
mov     dword [rsp+0x98], r12d
; ... later: call [rel 0x1400ac300] = RtlSetBits(&BitMapHeader, i-r12, 1)  <-- OOB write on final ULONG
; ... call [rel 0x1400aca38] = RtlFindSetBits / RtlClearBit on ctx+0x140
;
; PATCHED replacement (feature-gated fix):
; movzx r14d, ax
; call 0x140051978            ; Feature_NTFS_BR_TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRangeBitmapSizeAlignFix IsEnabled
; test eax, eax ; je <old path lea [r14+0x58]>
; lea rax, [r14+0x5b] ; and rax, 0xfffffffffffffffc   ; round total size up to ULONG (4-byte) boundary

breakpoints.why: Break at function entry. arg1(rcx)=IrpContext, arg2(rdx)=Scb/TOPS context (bitmap at rdx+0x140, mapping bitmaps at rdx+0x138/0x148), arg3(r8d)=range start bit, arg4(r9d)=range bit count. These four determine r14_3 and thus the undersized allocation.

Set a bp on the ExAllocatePoolWithTag call (call qword [rel 0x1400ad3f0], tag 0x3066744e). Inspect edx = requested size. Compute r14_3 = size*... ; if ((size-0x58)*8) % 32 == 16 the bitmap trailing ULONG overflows. Watch that rax (returned pool ptr) + size is the block end and the bitmap runs [rax+0x58 .. rax+0x58+(r14_3/8)).

breakpoints.address: resolve at runtime (call [rel 0x1400ac300] inside the function)
breakpoints.why: Break to observe the OOB write: RCX=&BitMapHeader (SizeOfBitMap=r14_3, Buffer=P+0x58), RDX=start index, R8=1. When start index maps into the final (partial) ULONG beyond P+0x58+(r14_3/8), the write lands in the next pool chunk.

watch: r8d/arg3 = TOPS range start bit, r9d/arg4 = TOPS range bit count (attacker-influenced via write offset/length)
r14_3 = (( (r15-1+r14) & ~0xf ) + 0x10) - (r14 & ~0xf)  — must satisfy r14_3 & 0x1f == 0x10 to overflow
edx at the ExAllocatePoolWithTag call = (r14_3>>3)+0x58 (unpatched) — the undersized size
r15 = returned 'Ntf0' pool pointer; bitmap buffer = r15+0x58; SizeOfBitMap word at r15+0x54
The ULONG at r15 + 0x58 + ((r14_3/8) & ~3) — this is the trailing ULONG that spills past the allocation
key_offsets: shr eax,0x3 ; add ax,0x58 @ ~0x1401038b8 — the unpatched size calc missing ULONG rounding (the exact removed/fixed instruction)
call qword [rel 0x1400ad3f0] (ExAllocatePoolWithTag, tag 0x3066744e) @ ~0x1401038d0 — allocation of the undersized bitmap record
mov word [r15+0x54], r14w @ ~0x140103905 — stores RTL_BITMAP.SizeOfBitMap = r14_3 bits
call qword [rel 0x1400ac300] (RtlSetBits) — OOB write sink; call qword [rel 0x1400aca38] (RtlFindSetBits) and RtlClearBit(ctx+0x140) — additional bitmap sinks
patched fix: lea rax,[r14+0x5b]; and rax,0xfffffffffffffffc — the added 4-byte alignment (present only in patched @ 0x140103808)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: As a standard user: 1) CreateTransaction (KTM). 2) CreateFileTransacted on an NTFS volume to open/create a file within the transaction (this arms the TxF TOPS logging path). 3) Issue WriteFile calls whose offsets/lengths dirty pages so NtfsCommonWrite -> TxfLogPriorToWrite -> TxfAllocateTopsRange -> TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange is invoked with a TOPS bit range where r14_3 & 0x1f == 0x10 (choose start bit r14 and count r15 so the 16-aligned span is 16/48/80/... bits). Ensure the entry RtlAreBitsClear(ctx+0x140, r14, arg4) returns FALSE (target bits already set) so the allocation branch is taken. Pre-groom nonpaged pool so a target object follows the 'Ntf0' chunk.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At RtlSetBits/RtlClearBit the write/read touches bytes at or past r15+0x58+(r14_3/8), i.e. beyond the ExAllocatePoolWithTag size in edx. With Special Pool / verifier enabled on tag 'Ntf0' (Ntf0), the OOB access faults immediately (DRIVER_VERIFIER_DETECTED_VIOLATION / PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA at the bitmap ULONG address). Without verifier, expect intermittent pool corruption BSODs (BAD_POOL_HEADER / 0x19, or KERNEL_MODE_HEAP_CORRUPTION) when the adjacent chunk is later freed. Compare against the patched build where edx is rounded up (…&~3) and the same input no longer overflows.
debugging_aids.notes: RTL_BITMAP layout here: SizeOfBitMap (ULONG) at struct+0x54 (P[0x2a]), Buffer at struct+0x58. The record header sets *P=1, dword[P+4]=0x15, word[P+0x56]=0x58, dword[P+0x50]=r12_1. Overflow only manifests when r14_3 is 16 mod 32 because Rtl bitmap ops round the bit count up to 32-bit ULONG granularity; multiples of 32 are safe (this is why the bug is intermittent). The fix is gated behind WIL feature Feature_NTFS_BR_TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRangeBitmapSizeAlignFix — on the unpatched binary the alignment is unconditionally absent. Distinguish vulnerable vs safe path by the `add ax,0x58` (vuln) vs `lea [r14+0x5b]; and ~3` (fixed) at the size computation.

summary: The patch rounds an RTL_BITMAP pool allocation up to a 32-bit ULONG boundary in ntfs.sys!TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange (Transactional NTFS TOPS flush). The pre-patch code allocates an 'Ntf0'-tagged buffer sized (SizeOfBitMap_bits/8)+0x58 without ULONG rounding, while SizeOfBitMap is always a multiple of 16; when the bit count is 16 mod 32 the trailing 32-bit bitmap word extends ~2 bytes past the logical allocation. This is a latent correctness/hardening bug (CWE-131), NOT the exploitable pool overflow the engine originally claimed: only RtlSetBits (single-bit, in-bounds) touches the undersized buffer (RtlFindSetBits/RtlClearBit/RtlAreBitsClear operate on a separate Scb bitmap at a2+0x140); the trailing-word read-modify-write preserves the out-of-bounds bytes (no attacker data written OOB); and 16-byte pool rounding absorbs the ~2 bytes on default systems, so no adjacent allocation is reached and nothing leaks to user mode. The access surfaces only as a fault under Special Pool / Driver Verifier on tag 'Ntf0'. Severity Low. The fix is STAGED behind the WIL feature flag Feature_NTFS_BR_TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRangeBitmapSizeAlignFix (old unrounded path retained when the flag is off; runtime default not statically determinable). An independent pass over the other 8 changed functions confirmed none adds or changes a bounds check, allocation size, copy length, loop bound, or input validation; the true fix is solely TxfSatisfyFlushForTopsRange.
